info.title: Strava API v3
info.description: The [Swagger Playground](https://developers.strava.com/playground) is the easiest way to familiarize yourself with the Strava API by submitting HTTP requests and observing the responses before you write any client code. It will show what a response will look like with different endpoints depending on the authorization scope you receive from your athletes. To use the Playground, go to https://www.strava.com/settings/api and change your “Authorization Callback Domain” to developers.strava.com. Please note, we only support Swagger 2.0. There is a known issue where you can only select one scope at a time. For more information, please check the section “client code” at https://developers.strava.com/docs.

get.description: Returns the currently authenticated athlete. Tokens with profile:read_all scope will receive a detailed athlete representation; all others will receive a summary representation.

200.description: Profile information for the authenticated athlete.

get.description: Returns the the authenticated athlete's heart rate and power zones. Requires profile:read_all.
Returns the activity stats of an athlete. Only includes data from activities set to Everyone visibilty.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the athlete. Must match the authenticated athlete.

get.description: Returns the activities of an athlete for a specific identifier. Requires activity:read. Only Me activities will be filtered out unless requested by a token with activity:read_all.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: An epoch timestamp to use for filtering activities that have taken place before a certain time.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: An epoch timestamp to use for filtering activities that have taken place after a certain time.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Number of items per page. Defaults to 30.

get.description:
Returns the given activity that is owned by the authenticated athlete. Requires activity:read for Everyone and Followers activities. Requires activity:read_all for Only Me activities.

We strongly encourage you to display the appropriate attribution that identifies Garmin as the data source and the device name in your application. Please see example below from VeloViewer (that provides an attribution for a Garmin Forerunner device).

![Attribution](/images/device-attribution-image.png)
Returns the given activity's streams. Requires activity:read scope. Requires activity:read_all scope for Only Me activities.
Summit Feature. Returns the zones of a given activity. Requires activity:read for Everyone and Followers activities. Requires activity:read_all for Only Me activities.

scopes.read: Read public segments, public routes, public profile data, public posts, public events, club feeds, and leaderboards
scopes.read_all: Read private routes, private segments, and private events for the user
scopes.profile:read_all: Read all profile information even if the user has set their profile visibility to Followers or Only You
scopes.profile:write: Update the user's weight and Functional Threshold Power (FTP), and access to star or unstar segments on their behalf
scopes.activity:read: Read the user's activity data for activities that are visible to Everyone and Followers, excluding privacy zone data
scopes.activity:read_all: The same access as activity:read, plus privacy zone data and access to read the user's activities with visibility set to Only You
scopes.activity:write: Access to create manual activities and uploads, and access to edit any activities that are visible to the app, based on activity read access level

Fault.description: Encapsulates the errors that may be returned from the API.

errors.description: The set of specific errors associated with this fault, if any.

ActivityStats.description: A set of rolled-up statistics and totals for an athlete

biggest_ride_distance.description: The longest distance ridden by the athlete.

biggest_climb_elevation_gain.description: The highest climb ridden by the athlete.

external_id.description: The identifier provided at upload time

upload_id.description: The identifier of the upload that resulted in this activity

moving_time.description: The activity's moving time, in seconds

elapsed_time.description: The activity's elapsed time, in seconds

elev_high.description: The activity's highest elevation, in meters

elev_low.description: The activity's lowest elevation, in meters

start_date.description: The time at which the activity was started.

start_date_local.description: The time at which the activity was started in the local timezone.

achievement_count.description: The number of achievements gained during this activity

kudos_count.description: The number of kudos given for this activity

comment_count.description: The number of comments for this activity

athlete_count.description: The number of athletes for taking part in a group activity

photo_count.description: The number of Instagram photos for this activity

total_photo_count.description: The number of Instagram and Strava photos for this activity

device_name.description: The name of the device used to record the activity

trainer.description: Whether this activity was recorded on a training machine

commute.description: Whether this activity is a commute

manual.description: Whether this activity was created manually

upload_id_str.description: The unique identifier of the upload in string format

average_speed.description: The activity's average speed, in meters per second

max_speed.description: The activity's max speed, in meters per second

has_kudoed.description: Whether the logged-in athlete has kudoed this activity

gear_id.description: The id of the gear for the activity

kilojoules.description: The total work done in kilojoules during this activity. Rides only

average_watts.description: Average power output in watts during this activity. Rides only

device_watts.description: Whether the watts are from a power meter, false if estimated

max_watts.description: Rides with power meter data only

weighted_average_watts.description: Similar to Normalized Power. Rides with power meter data only

calories.description: The number of kilocalories consumed during this activity

device_name.description: The name of the device used to record the activity

embed_token.description: The token used to embed a Strava activity

splits_metric.description: The splits of this activity in metric units (for runs)

splits_standard.description: The splits of this activity in imperial units (for runs)

resource_state.description: Resource state, indicates level of detail. Possible values: 1 -> "meta", 2 -> "summary", 3 -> "detail"

profile_medium.description: URL to a 62x62 pixel profile picture.

profile.description: URL to a 124x124 pixel profile picture.

state.description: The athlete's state or geographical region.

premium.description: Deprecated.  Use summit field instead. Whether the athlete has any Summit subscription.

summit.description: Whether the athlete has any Summit subscription.

created_at.description: The time at which the athlete was created.

updated_at.description: The time at which the athlete was last updated.

profile_medium.description: URL to a 60x60 pixel profile picture.

cover_photo.description: URL to a ~1185x580 pixel cover photo.

cover_photo_small.description: URL to a ~360x176  pixel cover photo.

activity_types.description: The activity types that count for a club. This takes precedence over sport_type.

state.description: The club's state or geographical region.

featured.description: Whether the club is featured or not.

verified.description: Whether the club is verified or not.

resource_state.description: Resource state, indicates level of detail. Possible values: 2 -> "summary", 3 -> "detail"

primary.description: Whether this gear's is the owner's default one.

distance.description: The distance logged with this gear.

code.description: The code associated with this error.

field.description: The specific field or aspect of the resource associated with this error.

resource.description: The type of resource associated with this error.

custom_zones.description: Whether the athlete has set their own custom heart rate zones

ActivityTotal.description: A roll-up of metrics pertaining to a set of activities. Values are in seconds and meters.

count.description: The number of activities considered in this total.

distance.description: The total distance covered by the considered activities.

moving_time.description: The total moving time of the considered activities.

elapsed_time.description: The total elapsed time of the considered activities.

elevation_gain.description: The total elevation gain of the considered activities.

achievement_count.description: The total number of achievements of the considered activities.

id.description: The unique identifier of the activity
The unique identifier of the athlete

ActivityType.description: An enumeration of the types an activity may have. Note that this enumeration does not include new sport types (e.g. MountainBikeRide, EMountainBikeRide), activities with these sport types will have the corresponding activity type (e.g. Ride for MountainBikeRide, EBikeRide for EMountainBikeRide)

SportType.description: An enumeration of the sport types an activity may have. Distinct from ActivityType in that it has new types (e.g. MountainBikeRide)

LatLng.description: A pair of latitude/longitude coordinates, represented as an array of 2 floating point numbers.

polyline.description: The polyline of the map, only returned on detailed representation of an object

summary_polyline.description: The summary polyline of the map

name.description: The name of the segment on which this effort was performed

start_index.description: The start index of this effort in its activity's stream

end_index.description: The end index of this effort in its activity's stream

average_watts.description: The average wattage of this effort

device_watts.description: For riding efforts, whether the wattage was reported by a dedicated recording device

average_heartrate.description: The heart heart rate of the athlete during this effort

max_heartrate.description: The maximum heart rate of the athlete during this effort

kom_rank.description: The rank of the effort on the global leaderboard if it belongs in the top 10 at the time of upload

pr_rank.description: The rank of the effort on the athlete's leaderboard if it belongs in the top 3 at the time of upload

hidden.description: Whether this effort should be hidden when viewed within an activity

average_speed.description: The average speed of this split, in meters per second

distance.description: The distance of this split, in meters

elapsed_time.description: The elapsed time of this split, in seconds

elevation_difference.description: The elevation difference of this split, in meters

pace_zone.description: The pacing zone of this split

moving_time.description: The moving time of this split, in seconds

id.description: The unique identifier of this lap

elapsed_time.description: The lap's elapsed time, in seconds

start_index.description: The start index of this effort in its activity's stream

end_index.description: The end index of this effort in its activity's stream

lap_index.description: The index of this lap in the activity it belongs to

max_speed.description: The maximum speed of this lat, in meters per second

moving_time.description: The lap's moving time, in seconds

pace_zone.description: The athlete's pace zone during this lap

start_date.description: The time at which the lap was started.

start_date_local.description: The time at which the lap was started in the local timezone.

total_elevation_gain.description: The elevation gain of this lap, in meters

data.description: The sequence of time values for this stream, in seconds
The sequence of distance values for this stream, in meters
The sequence of lat/long values for this stream
The sequence of altitude values for this stream, in meters
The sequence of velocity values for this stream, in meters per second
The sequence of heart rate values for this stream, in beats per minute
The sequence of cadence values for this stream, in rotations per minute
The sequence of power values for this stream, in watts
The sequence of temperature values for this stream, in celsius degrees
The sequence of moving values for this stream, as boolean values
The sequence of grade values for this stream, as percents of a grade

resource_state.description: Resource state, indicates level of detail. Possible values: 1 -> "meta", 2 -> "summary", 3 -> "detail"

id.description: The unique identifier of this effort

activity_id.description: The unique identifier of the activity related to this effort

start_date.description: The time at which the effort was started.

start_date_local.description: The time at which the effort was started in the local timezone.

is_kom.description: Whether this effort is the current best on the leaderboard

id.description: The unique identifier of this segment

average_grade.description: The segment's average grade, in percents

maximum_grade.description: The segments's maximum grade, in percents

elevation_high.description: The segments's highest elevation, in meters

elevation_low.description: The segments's lowest elevation, in meters

climb_category.description: The category of the climb [0, 5]. Higher is harder ie. 5 is Hors catégorie, 0 is uncategorized in climb_category.

state.description: The segments's state or geographical region.

original_size.description: The number of data points in this stream

resolution.description: The level of detail (sampling) in which this stream was returned

series_type.description: The base series used in the case the stream was downsampled

min.description: The minimum value in the range.

max.description: The maximum value in the range.

pr_activity_id.description: The unique identifier of the activity related to the PR effort.

pr_elapsed_time.description: The elapsed time ot the PR effort.

pr_date.description: The time at which the PR effort was started.

effort_count.description: Number of efforts by the authenticated athlete on this segment.

info.title: Strava API v3
info.description: The [Swagger Playground](https://developers.strava.com/playground) is the easiest way to familiarize yourself with the Strava API by submitting HTTP requests and observing the responses before you write any client code. It will show what a response will look like with different endpoints depending on the authorization scope you receive from your athletes. To use the Playground, go to https://www.strava.com/settings/api and change your “Authorization Callback Domain” to developers.strava.com. Please note, we only support Swagger 2.0. There is a known issue where you can only select one scope at a time. For more information, please check the section “client code” at https://developers.strava.com/docs.
